---
title: 'Galvano: dissertation Ch. 2 stats'
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-01"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
# --- core data / tidy tools ---
library(tidyverse)   # includes dplyr, readr, tidyr, tibble, ggplot2, etc.

# --- mixed models ---
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)

# --- post-hoc / inference ---
library(emmeans)
library(pbkrtest)

# --- model effects / R² ---
library(effects)
library(MuMIn)
library(performance)
```

# Load in datasets
```{r wd1}
# moments data
all_data <- read_csv("moments_FULL_REVISION_all_w-demog_CLEAN_02-01-26.csv")
all_data <- all_data %>%
  rename(
    word = words,
  )
all_data
sum(is.na(all_data$COG))

MPA <- read_csv("MPA_FULL_REVISION_all_w-demog_CLEAN_02-01-26.csv")
MPA <- MPA %>%
  rename(
    word = words,
  )
MPA
sum(is.na(MPA$Fm))

norm_formants <- read_csv("formants_FULL_REVISION_NORMED_CLEAN_all_w-demog_02-02-26.csv")
norm_formants <- norm_formants %>%
  rename(
    word = words,
  )
norm_formants <- norm_formants %>%
  rename(
    F1_norm = `F*1`,
    F2_norm = `F*2`,
    F3_norm = `F*3`,
    segment = phones_short
  )
length(unique(norm_formants$speaker))
```

# Set up stepwise model selection
```{r stepwise}
# -------------------------
# helpers used by stepwise + rank-fix
# -------------------------
capture_fit <- function(expr) {
  msgs  <- character()
  warns <- character()

  # withCallingHandlers() lets you intercept messages and warnings while
  # continuing evaluation; invokeRestart("muffleMessage"/"muffleWarning")
  # suppresses printing after capture.
  # R condition handling docs:
  # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/base/html/conditions.html
  val <- withCallingHandlers(
    expr,
    message = function(m) {
      msgs  <<- c(msgs, conditionMessage(m))
      invokeRestart("muffleMessage")
    },
    warning = function(w) {
      warns <<- c(warns, conditionMessage(w))
      invokeRestart("muffleWarning")
    }
  )

  list(value = val, messages = msgs, warnings = warns)
}

fit_with_logs <- function(mod, new_form) {
  # tryCatch() traps errors and returns the error object here instead of
  # stopping execution, while capture_fit() separately collects messages/warnings.
  # R condition handling docs:
  # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/base/html/conditions.html
  res <- capture_fit(tryCatch(update(mod, new_form), error = function(e) e))
  list(fit = res$value, messages = res$messages, warnings = res$warnings)
}

rank_diag <- function(mod) {
  # model.frame() reconstructs the data used by the fitted model.
  # model.matrix() builds the fixed-effects design matrix.
  # R model.frame / model.matrix docs:
  # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/stats/html/model.frame.html
  # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/stats/html/model.matrix.html
  mf <- model.frame(mod)

  # nobars() removes random-effects terms from an lme4-style formula so that
  # the fixed-effects design matrix can be inspected directly.
  # lme4 package manual:
  # https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/lme4/lme4.pdf
  #
  # Note: recent package news indicates these formula helpers are being moved
  # toward the reformulas package, so reformulas::nobars() may be the more
  # future-proof choice in newer workflows.
  # reformulas package:
  # https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/reformulas/reformulas.pdf
  ff <- lme4::nobars(formula(mod))

  X  <- model.matrix(ff, data = mf)

  # qr() gives the QR decomposition used here to diagnose rank deficiency.
  # R qr docs:
  # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/base/html/qr.html
  qrX <- qr(X)
  dropped_n <- ncol(X) - qrX$rank

  if (dropped_n <= 0) {
    return(list(dropped_n = 0L, dropped_cols = character(0), aliased = NULL))
  }

  keep_idx <- qrX$pivot[seq_len(qrX$rank)]
  dropped_cols <- colnames(X)[setdiff(seq_len(ncol(X)), keep_idx)]

  # alias() identifies complete aliasing / non-estimable linear dependencies
  # in model terms. lm.fit() is used here as a lightweight linear-model engine
  # over the already-built design matrix.
  # R alias docs:
  # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/stats/html/alias.html
  ali <- tryCatch(
    alias(stats::lm.fit(X, rep(0, nrow(X))))$Complete,
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  list(dropped_n = dropped_n, dropped_cols = dropped_cols, aliased = ali)
}

print_rank_diag <- function(diag, indent = "  ") {
  if (is.null(diag) || diag$dropped_n == 0L) return(invisible(NULL))

  cat(indent, "Rank deficiency:", diag$dropped_n, "column(s) would be dropped\n", sep = "")
  if (length(diag$dropped_cols)) {
    cat(indent, "Dropped design-matrix columns:\n", sep = "")
    cat(paste0(indent, indent, "- ", diag$dropped_cols), sep = "\n")
    cat("\n")
  }
  if (!is.null(diag$aliased)) {
    cat(indent, "Aliasing (why):\n", sep = "")
    print(diag$aliased)
  }
  invisible(NULL)
}

violates_hierarchy <- function(all_terms, dropped) {
  kept <- setdiff(all_terms, dropped)
  inter <- kept[grepl(":", kept, fixed = TRUE)]
  if (!length(inter)) return(FALSE)

  dropped_set <- unique(dropped)
  for (it in inter) {
    # strsplit() is used to split interaction terms like A:B into component terms.
    # R strsplit docs:
    # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/base/html/strsplit.html
    parts <- strsplit(it, ":", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
    if (any(parts %in% dropped_set)) return(TRUE)
  }
  FALSE
}

# -------------------------
# STEPWISE BEST-SUBSET (prints all candidates)
# -------------------------
stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all <- function(
  model,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = c("AIC", "BIC"),
  verbose = TRUE,
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = Inf,
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
) {
  # match.arg() validates the user's choice against allowed options
  # and returns the matched value.
  # R match.arg docs:
  # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/base/html/match.arg.html
  criterion <- match.arg(criterion)

  current <- model

  # AIC / BIC are standard information criteria for model comparison.
  # R AIC / BIC docs:
  # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/stats/html/AIC.html
  current_score <- if (criterion == "AIC") AIC(current) else BIC(current)

  hist <- list()
  step <- 1

  if (verbose) {
    cat("\nSTART:", criterion, "=", round(current_score, 3), "\n")
    cat("Keeping (never drop):", if (length(keep_terms)) paste(keep_terms, collapse = ", ") else "none", "\n")
    cat("Current formula:\n"); print(formula(current))

    # rank_diag() and print_rank_diag() are custom helpers defined earlier
    # to diagnose rank deficiency / aliasing in the fixed-effects design matrix.
    diag0 <- rank_diag(current)
    if (diag0$dropped_n > 0) {
      cat("Current model rank-diagnostics:\n")
      print_rank_diag(diag0, indent = "  ")
      cat("\n")
    }
  }

  repeat {
    if (step > max_steps) {
      if (verbose) cat("\nReached max_steps. Stopping.\n")
      break
    }

    # nobars() removes random-effects terms from the mixed-effects formula,
    # and terms(..., "term.labels") extracts the fixed-effect term labels.
    # lme4 / reformulas formula helpers:
    # https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/lme4/lme4.pdf
    # https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/reformulas/reformulas.pdf
    # R terms docs:
    # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/stats/html/terms.formula.html
    all_terms <- attr(terms(lme4::nobars(formula(current))), "term.labels")
    droppable <- setdiff(all_terms, keep_terms)

    if (!length(droppable)) {
      if (verbose) cat("\nNo eligible terms to drop. Stopping.\n")
      break
    }

    max_k <- min(length(droppable), max_drop_size)

    if (verbose) {
      cat("\n==============================\n")
      cat("STEP", step, "\n")
      cat("==============================\n")
      cat("Current", criterion, "=", round(current_score, 3), "\n")
      cat("Eligible droppable terms (k up to", max_k, "):\n  - ",
          paste(droppable, collapse = "\n  - "), "\n", sep = "")
    }

    best_drop  <- character(0)
    best_score <- current_score
    best_fit   <- NULL
    best_msgs  <- character()
    best_warns <- character()
    best_diag  <- NULL

    cand_table <- list()
    cand_i <- 0L

    for (k in 1:max_k) {
      # combn() generates all subsets of size k from the droppable terms.
      # R combn docs:
      # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/utils/html/combn.html
      combos <- combn(droppable, k, simplify = FALSE)

      if (verbose) cat("\n-- Testing drops of size k =", k, " (", length(combos), " candidates) --\n", sep = "")

      for (cs in combos) {
        # violates_hierarchy() is a custom helper that prevents keeping
        # an interaction term if one of its lower-order components is dropped.
        if (enforce_hierarchy && violates_hierarchy(all_terms, cs)) {
          if (verbose) {
            cat("\n--- Candidate drop:", paste(cs, collapse = ", "), "\n")
            cat("SKIP (hierarchy violation)\n")
          }
          next
        }

        # Build an update formula by subtracting the candidate drop set.
        # update.formula() applies incremental modifications to formulas.
        # R update.formula docs:
        # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/stats/html/update.formula.html
        upd <- paste(". ~ .", paste(sprintf("- `%s`", cs), collapse = " "))
        new_form <- update(formula(current), as.formula(upd))

        # fit_with_logs() is a custom helper defined earlier that:
        #   1) updates/refits the model
        #   2) captures messages/warnings
        #   3) returns errors without stopping execution
        res <- fit_with_logs(current, new_form)

        # lme4 mixed-model fits inherit from class "merMod"
        # lme4 package manual:
        # https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/lme4/lme4.pdf
        ok <- inherits(res$fit, "merMod")
        sc <- if (ok) (if (criterion == "AIC") AIC(res$fit) else BIC(res$fit)) else NA_real_
        diag <- if (ok) rank_diag(res$fit) else NULL

        cand_i <- cand_i + 1L
        cand_table[[cand_i]] <- data.frame(
          step = step,
          n_drop = k,
          dropped = paste(cs, collapse = ", "),
          score = sc,
          ok = ok,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )

        if (verbose) {
          cat("\n--- Candidate drop:", paste(cs, collapse = ", "), "\n")
          cat("Formula:", deparse(new_form), "\n")
          if (!ok) {
            cat("FIT ERROR (skipped)\n")
          } else {
            cat(criterion, "=", round(sc, 3), "\n")
            print_rank_diag(diag, indent = "  ")
          }
          if (length(res$messages)) {
            cat("Messages:\n")
            cat(paste0("  - ", res$messages), sep = "\n")
            cat("\n")
          }
          if (length(res$warnings)) {
            cat("Warnings:\n")
            cat(paste0("  - ", res$warnings), sep = "\n")
            cat("\n")
          }
        }

        # Keep the best strictly improving candidate under the chosen criterion.
        if (ok && is.finite(sc) && sc < best_score - 1e-8) {
          best_score <- sc
          best_drop  <- cs
          best_fit   <- res$fit
          best_msgs  <- res$messages
          best_warns <- res$warnings
          best_diag  <- diag
        }
      }
    }

    cand_df <- if (length(cand_table)) do.call(rbind, cand_table) else NULL
    if (!is.null(cand_df)) cand_df <- cand_df[order(cand_df$score), ]

    if (is.null(best_fit)) {
      if (verbose) cat("\nNo candidate improved", criterion, ". Stopping.\n")
      break
    }

    if (verbose) {
      cat("\n*** CHOSEN (best improving", criterion, ") ***\n")
      cat("Best drop set:", paste(best_drop, collapse = ", "), "\n")
      cat(criterion, "=", round(best_score, 3), "\n")
      cat("New formula:\n"); cat(deparse(formula(best_fit)), "\n")
      if (!is.null(best_diag) && best_diag$dropped_n > 0) {
        cat("Rank-diagnostics for CHOSEN model:\n")
        print_rank_diag(best_diag, indent = "  ")
        cat("\n")
      }
    }

    # Save stepwise search history, including candidate table and rank diagnostics.
    hist[[step]] <- list(
      step = step,
      dropped = best_drop,
      score = best_score,
      formula = deparse(formula(best_fit)),
      rank_dropped_n = if (!is.null(best_diag)) best_diag$dropped_n else NA_integer_,
      rank_dropped_cols = if (!is.null(best_diag)) best_diag$dropped_cols else character(0),
      rank_alias = if (!is.null(best_diag)) best_diag$aliased else NULL,
      messages = best_msgs,
      warnings = best_warns,
      candidates = cand_df
    )

    current <- best_fit
    current_score <- best_score
    step <- step + 1
  }

  # Store the full search history as an attribute on the returned fitted model.
  # R attributes docs:
  # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/base/html/attr.html
  attr(current, "stepwise_history") <- hist
  current
}

# -------------------------
# OPTIONAL: minimal rank-fix after stepwise
# -------------------------
is_full_rank <- function(mod) {
  # fixef() extracts the fixed-effect coefficient estimates from an lme4 model.
  # If the design matrix is rank deficient, some coefficients are returned as NA.
  # lme4 fixef docs:
  # https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/lme4/lme4.pdf
  fe <- lme4::fixef(mod)

  # anyNA() checks whether any coefficient estimates are NA
  # (a simple indicator of rank deficiency).
  # R anyNA docs:
  # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/base/html/anyNA.html
  !anyNA(fe)
}

minimal_rank_fix_after_stepwise <- function(
  model,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  max_k = 4,
  verbose = TRUE
) {

  # If the model already has a full-rank fixed-effects design matrix,
  # return it unchanged.
  if (is_full_rank(model)) {
    if (verbose) cat("\nModel is already full rank. No constraint fixes needed.\n")
    return(model)
  }

  # Extract fixed-effect term labels from the model formula
  # (removing random-effects terms first).
  # lme4::nobars removes random-effects terms from the formula.
  # R terms() extracts term labels.
  # docs:
  # https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/lme4/lme4.pdf
  # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/stats/html/terms.formula.html
  all_terms <- attr(terms(lme4::nobars(formula(model))), "term.labels")

  # Do not allow dropping terms that were explicitly protected.
  terms <- setdiff(all_terms, keep_terms)

  if (verbose) {
    cat("\nConstraint stage: starting model is rank deficient.\n")
    cat("Eligible additional drop terms:\n")
    print(terms)
    cat("Starting formula:\n"); print(formula(model))
  }

  tested_all <- list()

  # Try dropping increasingly large subsets of terms until
  # a full-rank model is found.
  for (k in 1:min(max_k, length(terms))) {

    if (verbose) {
      cat("\n====================================\n")
      cat("CONSTRAINT SEARCH: additional drops of size k =", k, "\n")
      cat("====================================\n")
    }

    # Generate all combinations of size k
    # R combn docs:
    # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/utils/html/combn.html
    combos <- combn(terms, k, simplify = FALSE)

    hits <- list()

    for (cs in combos) {

      # Update formula by subtracting the candidate drop terms.
      # R update.formula docs:
      # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/stats/html/update.formula.html
      new_form <- update(
        formula(model),
        paste(". ~ . -", paste(cs, collapse = " - "))
      )

      # fit_with_logs() is the helper defined earlier that:
      #  • refits the model
      #  • captures warnings/messages
      #  • prevents the search from stopping on errors
      res <- fit_with_logs(model, new_form)

      # lme4 models inherit from class "merMod"
      ok <- inherits(res$fit, "merMod")

      if (verbose) {
        cat("\n--- Candidate additional drops:", paste(cs, collapse = ", "), "\n")
        cat("Formula:", deparse(new_form), "\n")
      }

      if (!ok) {
        if (verbose) cat("FIT ERROR (skipped)\n")
        next
      }

      # Check if the resulting model is full rank
      fr <- is_full_rank(res$fit)

      # Compute model AIC
      # R AIC docs:
      # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/stats/html/AIC.html
      aic <- AIC(res$fit)

      if (verbose) cat("AIC:", round(aic, 3), "| Full rank:", fr, "\n")

      rec <- list(
        dropped = cs,
        AIC = aic,
        full_rank = fr,
        formula = deparse(formula(res$fit)),
        model = res$fit
      )

      tested_all[[length(tested_all) + 1]] <- rec
      if (fr) hits[[length(hits) + 1]] <- rec
    }

    # If any full-rank models were found at this subset size,
    # choose the one with the best (lowest) AIC.
    if (length(hits)) {

      best <- hits[[which.min(vapply(hits, `[[`, numeric(1), "AIC"))]]

      if (verbose) {
        cat("\n*** CHOSEN FULL-RANK MODEL (minimal k =", k, ") ***\n")
        cat("Additional drops:", paste(best$dropped, collapse = ", "), "\n")
        cat("AIC:", round(best$AIC, 3), "\n")
        cat("Final formula:\n"); cat(best$formula, "\n")
      }

      # Store search diagnostics as attributes on the returned model
      # so the user can inspect all tested candidates later.
      attr(best$model, "rankfix_candidates") <- hits
      attr(best$model, "rankfix_tested_all") <- tested_all

      return(best$model)
    }
  }

  stop("No full-rank model found with up to k = ", max_k, " additional drops.")
}
```

# Select models
## COG
```{r finmodcog}
exclude_speakers <- c("S18", "S44", "S09", "S10")

all_data_clean <- all_data %>%
  filter(!speaker %in% exclude_speakers)

length(unique(all_data_clean$speaker))


# ----------------------------
# 0) Helper: sum-code factors
# ----------------------------
sum_code <- function(df, cols) {

  # Apply sum (deviation) coding to selected categorical predictors.
  # Sum coding constrains the coefficients so that category effects
  # sum to zero, making each coefficient represent a deviation from
  # the grand mean rather than from a reference level.
  # R contr.sum docs:
  # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/stats/html/contr.sum.html

  for (nm in cols) {

    # Ensure the column is treated as a factor and drop unused levels.
    # droplevels() removes factor levels not present in the data.
    # R droplevels docs:
    # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/base/html/droplevels.html
    df[[nm]] <- droplevels(factor(df[[nm]]))

    # Assign sum contrasts to the factor.
    # contrasts() sets the contrast matrix used by model.matrix()
    # when constructing the design matrix.
    # R contrasts docs:
    # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/stats/html/contrasts.html
    contrasts(df[[nm]]) <- contr.sum(nlevels(df[[nm]]))
  }

  df
}

# ----------------------------
# 1) Build modeling dataset
# ----------------------------
make_md_cc <- function(all_data_clean) {

  # Compute global medians for three scalar predictors.
  # median() computes the median while optionally ignoring NA values.
  # R median docs:
  # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/stats/html/median.html
  global_masc_med  <- median(as.numeric(all_data_clean$masc), na.rm = TRUE)
  global_femme_med <- median(as.numeric(all_data_clean$femme), na.rm = TRUE)
  global_gn_med    <- median(as.numeric(all_data_clean$gender_normative), na.rm = TRUE)

  md <- all_data_clean %>%
    mutate(
      # Ensure predictors are numeric before transformation.
      masc  = as.numeric(masc),
      femme = as.numeric(femme),
      gender_normative = as.numeric(gender_normative),

      # Global-median imputation for missing values in these three predictors.
      # if_else() performs vectorized conditional replacement.
      # R if_else docs:
      # https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/if_else.html
      masc  = if_else(is.na(masc),  global_masc_med,  masc),
      femme = if_else(is.na(femme), global_femme_med, femme),
      gender_normative = if_else(is.na(gender_normative), global_gn_med, gender_normative)
    ) %>%
    transmute(
      # Keep only variables used in modeling and create derived ones.
      COG,
      logCOG = log(COG),   # log transform of spectral center-of-gravity
      duration,

      # Linguistic predictors
      segment,
      prev_POA,
      nxt_POA,

      # Social categorical predictors
      task,
      place_origin_cut,
      L1s_cut,
      socio_econ_cut,
      race_ethn_cut,
      sexuality_gender_cut,

      # Numeric predictors (imputed above)
      masc,
      femme,
      gender_normative,

      # Grouping factors for mixed-effects model
      word,
      speaker
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      # Z-score numeric predictors (mean=0, sd=1) for comparability
      # scale() standardizes numeric vectors.
      # R scale docs:
      # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/base/html/scale.html
      z_dur   = as.numeric(scale(duration)),
      z_masc  = as.numeric(scale(masc)),
      z_femme = as.numeric(scale(femme)),
      z_gn    = as.numeric(scale(gender_normative)),

      # Ensure grouping variables are factors with unused levels removed
      # droplevels() removes factor levels not present in the data.
      # R droplevels docs:
      # https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/base/html/droplevels.html
      word    = droplevels(factor(word)),
      speaker = droplevels(factor(speaker))
    ) %>%
    droplevels()

  # Apply sum (deviation) coding to categorical fixed effects.
  # sum_code() is the helper defined earlier that assigns contr.sum contrasts.
  factor_cols <- c(
    "segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","task",
    "place_origin_cut","L1s_cut","socio_econ_cut",
    "race_ethn_cut","sexuality_gender_cut"
  )
  md <- sum_code(md, factor_cols)

  # Variables required for model fitting.
  vars_needed <- c(
    "logCOG","z_dur","z_masc","z_femme","z_gn",
    "segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","task",
    "place_origin_cut","L1s_cut","socio_econ_cut","race_ethn_cut","sexuality_gender_cut",
    "word","speaker"
  )

  # Remove rows with missing values in required predictors.
  # drop_na() removes rows with NA values in specified columns.
  # tidyr drop_na docs:
  # https://tidyr.tidyverse.org/reference/drop_na.html
  md %>%
    tidyr::drop_na(dplyr::all_of(vars_needed)) %>%
    droplevels()
}

# Build modeling dataset
md_cc <- make_md_cc(all_data_clean)

# Number of unique speakers in the resulting dataset
# unique() returns distinct values.
# length() counts them.
length(unique(md_cc$speaker))


# ----------------------------------------
# 3) lx-only model
# ----------------------------------------
mod1_full <- lmer(
  logCOG ~ segment + prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod1_final <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod1_full,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = "AIC",
  verbose = TRUE, # set TRUE if you want EVERY candidate printed
)

anova(mod1_final)

# ----------------------------------------
# 4) full model
# ----------------------------------------

mod2_full <- lmer(
  logCOG ~ segment + prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    task + z_masc + z_femme + z_gn + segment * sexuality_gender_cut +
    race_ethn_cut + L1s_cut + place_origin_cut + socio_econ_cut + 
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod_step <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod2_full,
  keep_terms = c("segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","z_dur"),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod_step)
saveRDS(mod_step, "mod_step_COG_all.rds") 

mod_step <- readRDS("mod_step_COG_all.rds") 
mod_step
r2_nakagawa(mod_step)


### /s/-only ###
md_cc_s <- md_cc[md_cc$segment == "S", ]
length(unique(md_cc_s$speaker))

mod1_full1 <- lmer(
  logCOG ~ prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc_s,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod1_final1 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod1_full1,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod1_final1)


mod_cog_s <- lmer(logCOG ~ prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    task + z_masc + z_femme + z_gn + sexuality_gender_cut +
    race_ethn_cut + L1s_cut + place_origin_cut + socio_econ_cut + 
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc_s,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod_step_s <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod_cog_s,
  keep_terms = c("prev_POA","nxt_POA","z_dur"),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod_step_s)
saveRDS(mod_step_s, "mod_step_COG_s.rds") 

mod_step_s <- readRDS("mod_step_COG_s.rds") 
mod_step_s
r2_nakagawa(mod_step_s)
```

## SD
```{r finmodsd}
length(unique(all_data_clean$speaker))

# ----------------------------
# 1) Build modeling dataset
# ----------------------------
make_md_cc1 <- function(all_data_clean) {

  # global medians for the three scalar predictors
  global_masc_med  <- median(as.numeric(all_data_clean$masc), na.rm = TRUE)
  global_femme_med <- median(as.numeric(all_data_clean$femme), na.rm = TRUE)
  global_gn_med    <- median(as.numeric(all_data_clean$gender_normative), na.rm = TRUE)

  md <- all_data_clean %>%
    mutate(
      # coerce to numeric
      masc  = as.numeric(masc),
      femme = as.numeric(femme),
      gender_normative = as.numeric(gender_normative),

      # global-median imputation (ONLY these three)
      masc  = if_else(is.na(masc),  global_masc_med,  masc),
      femme = if_else(is.na(femme), global_femme_med, femme),
      gender_normative = if_else(is.na(gender_normative), global_gn_med, gender_normative)
    ) %>%
    transmute(
      SD,
      logSD = log(SD),
      duration,

      # linguistic
      segment,
      prev_POA,
      nxt_POA,

      # social (categorical)
      task,
      place_origin_cut,
      L1s_cut,
      socio_econ_cut,
      race_ethn_cut,
      sexuality_gender_cut,

      # numeric-only predictors (imputed)
      masc,
      femme,
      gender_normative,

      # grouping factors
      word,
      speaker
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      # z-score numeric-only
      z_dur   = as.numeric(scale(duration)),
      z_masc  = as.numeric(scale(masc)),
      z_femme = as.numeric(scale(femme)),
      z_gn    = as.numeric(scale(gender_normative)),

      word    = droplevels(factor(word)),
      speaker = droplevels(factor(speaker))
    ) %>%
    droplevels()

  # sum-code fixed-effect factors (NOT word/speaker)
  factor_cols <- c(
    "segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","task",
    "place_origin_cut","L1s_cut","socio_econ_cut",
    "race_ethn_cut","sexuality_gender_cut"
  )
  md <- sum_code(md, factor_cols)

  # rows required for modeling
  vars_needed <- c(
    "logSD","z_dur","z_masc","z_femme","z_gn",
    "segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","task",
    "place_origin_cut","L1s_cut","socio_econ_cut","race_ethn_cut","sexuality_gender_cut",
    "word","speaker"
  )

  md %>% tidyr::drop_na(dplyr::all_of(vars_needed)) %>% droplevels()
}

md_cc1 <- make_md_cc1(all_data_clean)

length(unique(md_cc1$speaker))


# ----------------------------------------
# 3) lx-only model
# ----------------------------------------
mod3_full <- lmer(
  logSD ~ segment + prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc1,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod3_final <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod3_full,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = "AIC",
  verbose = TRUE, # set TRUE if you want EVERY candidate printed
)

anova(mod3_final)

# ----------------------------------------
# 4) full model
# ----------------------------------------

mod4_full <- lmer(
  logSD ~ segment + prev_POA + z_dur +
    task + z_masc + z_femme + z_gn + segment * sexuality_gender_cut +
    race_ethn_cut + L1s_cut +
    place_origin_cut + socio_econ_cut + 
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc1,
  REML = FALSE
)
anova(mod4_full)

mod_step1 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod4_full,
  keep_terms = c("segment","prev_POA","z_dur"),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod_step1)
saveRDS(mod_step1, "mod_step_SD_all.rds") 

mod_step1 <- readRDS("mod_step_SD_all.rds") 
mod_step1
r2_nakagawa(mod_step1)


### /s/-only ###
md_cc1_s <- md_cc1[md_cc1$segment == "S", ]
length(unique(md_cc1_s$speaker))

mod2_full1 <- lmer(
  logSD ~ prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc1_s,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod2_final1 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod2_full1,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod2_final1)


mod_sd_s <- lmer(logSD ~ prev_POA + z_dur +
    task + z_masc + z_femme + z_gn + sexuality_gender_cut +
    race_ethn_cut + L1s_cut + place_origin_cut + socio_econ_cut + 
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc1_s,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod_step_s1 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod_sd_s,
  keep_terms = c("prev_POA","z_dur"),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod_step_s1)
saveRDS(mod_step_s1, "mod_step_SD_s.rds") 

mod_step_s1 <- readRDS("mod_step_SD_s.rds") 
mod_step_s1
r2_nakagawa(mod_step_s1)
```

## skew

```{r finmodskew}
make_md_cc2 <- function(all_data_clean) {

  # global medians for the three scalar predictors
  global_masc_med  <- median(as.numeric(all_data_clean$masc), na.rm = TRUE)
  global_femme_med <- median(as.numeric(all_data_clean$femme), na.rm = TRUE)
  global_gn_med    <- median(as.numeric(all_data_clean$gender_normative), na.rm = TRUE)

  md <- all_data_clean %>%
    mutate(
      # coerce to numeric
      masc  = as.numeric(masc),
      femme = as.numeric(femme),
      gender_normative = as.numeric(gender_normative),

      # global-median imputation (ONLY these three)
      masc  = if_else(is.na(masc),  global_masc_med,  masc),
      femme = if_else(is.na(femme), global_femme_med, femme),
      gender_normative = if_else(is.na(gender_normative), global_gn_med, gender_normative)
    ) %>%
    transmute(
      skew,
      duration,

      # linguistic
      segment,
      prev_POA,
      nxt_POA,

      # social (categorical)
      task,
      place_origin_cut,
      L1s_cut,
      socio_econ_cut,
      race_ethn_cut,
      sexuality_gender_cut,

      # numeric-only predictors (imputed)
      masc,
      femme,
      gender_normative,

      # grouping factors
      word,
      speaker
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      # z-score numeric-only
      z_dur   = as.numeric(scale(duration)),
      z_masc  = as.numeric(scale(masc)),
      z_femme = as.numeric(scale(femme)),
      z_gn    = as.numeric(scale(gender_normative)),

      word    = droplevels(factor(word)),
      speaker = droplevels(factor(speaker))
    ) %>%
    droplevels()

  # sum-code fixed-effect factors (NOT word/speaker)
  factor_cols <- c(
    "segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","task",
    "place_origin_cut","L1s_cut","socio_econ_cut",
    "race_ethn_cut","sexuality_gender_cut"
  )
  md <- sum_code(md, factor_cols)

  # must-have vars for modeling
  vars_needed <- c(
    "skew","z_dur","z_masc","z_femme","z_gn",
    "segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","task",
    "place_origin_cut","L1s_cut","socio_econ_cut","race_ethn_cut","sexuality_gender_cut",
    "word","speaker"
  )

  md %>% tidyr::drop_na(dplyr::all_of(vars_needed)) %>% droplevels()
}

md_cc2 <- make_md_cc2(all_data_clean)

length(unique(md_cc1$speaker))


# ----------------------------------------
# 3) lx-only model
# ----------------------------------------
mod4_full <- lmer(
  skew ~ segment + prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc2,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod4_final <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod4_full,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod4_final)

# ----------------------------------------
# 4) full model
# ----------------------------------------

mod4_full1 <- lmer(
  skew ~ segment + prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    task + z_masc + z_femme + z_gn + segment * sexuality_gender_cut +
    race_ethn_cut + L1s_cut +
    place_origin_cut + socio_econ_cut + 
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc2,
  REML = FALSE
)
anova(mod4_full1)

mod_step2 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod4_full1,
  keep_terms = c("segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","z_dur"),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod_step2)
saveRDS(mod_step2, "mod_step_skew_all.rds") 
r2_nakagawa(mod_step2)


### /s/-only ###
md_cc2_s <- md_cc2[md_cc2$segment == "S", ]
length(unique(md_cc2_s$speaker))

mod3_full1 <- lmer(
  skew ~ prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc2_s,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod3_final1 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod3_full1,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod3_final1)


mod_skew_s <- lmer(skew ~ prev_POA + z_dur +
    task + z_masc + z_femme + z_gn + sexuality_gender_cut +
    race_ethn_cut + L1s_cut + place_origin_cut + socio_econ_cut + 
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc2_s,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod_step_s2 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod_skew_s,
  keep_terms = c("prev_POA","z_dur"),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod_step_s2)
saveRDS(mod_step_s2, "mod_step_skew_s.rds") 

mod_step_s2 <- readRDS("mod_step_skew_s.rds") 
mod_step_s2
r2_nakagawa(mod_step_s2)
```

## duration

```{r finmoddur}
make_md_cc3 <- function(all_data_clean) {

  # global medians for the three scalar predictors
  global_masc_med  <- median(as.numeric(all_data_clean$masc), na.rm = TRUE)
  global_femme_med <- median(as.numeric(all_data_clean$femme), na.rm = TRUE)
  global_gn_med    <- median(as.numeric(all_data_clean$gender_normative), na.rm = TRUE)

  md <- all_data_clean %>%
    mutate(
      # coerce to numeric
      masc  = as.numeric(masc),
      femme = as.numeric(femme),
      gender_normative = as.numeric(gender_normative),

      # global-median imputation (ONLY these three)
      masc  = if_else(is.na(masc),  global_masc_med,  masc),
      femme = if_else(is.na(femme), global_femme_med, femme),
      gender_normative = if_else(is.na(gender_normative), global_gn_med, gender_normative)
    ) %>%
    transmute(
      duration,
      logdur = log(duration),
      
      # linguistic
      segment,
      prev_POA,
      nxt_POA,

      # social (categorical)
      task,
      place_origin_cut,
      L1s_cut,
      socio_econ_cut,
      race_ethn_cut,
      sexuality_gender_cut,

      # numeric-only predictors (imputed)
      masc,
      femme,
      gender_normative,

      # grouping factors
      word,
      speaker
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      # z-score numeric-only
      z_logdur = as.numeric(scale(logdur)),
      z_masc  = as.numeric(scale(masc)),
      z_femme = as.numeric(scale(femme)),
      z_gn    = as.numeric(scale(gender_normative)),

      word    = droplevels(factor(word)),
      speaker = droplevels(factor(speaker))
    ) %>%
    droplevels()

  # sum-code fixed-effect factors (NOT word/speaker)
  factor_cols <- c(
    "segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","task",
    "place_origin_cut","L1s_cut","socio_econ_cut",
    "race_ethn_cut","sexuality_gender_cut"
  )
  md <- sum_code(md, factor_cols)

  # must-have vars for modeling
  vars_needed <- c(
    "z_logdur","z_masc","z_femme","z_gn",
    "segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","task",
    "place_origin_cut","L1s_cut","socio_econ_cut","race_ethn_cut","sexuality_gender_cut",
    "word","speaker"
  )

  md %>% tidyr::drop_na(dplyr::all_of(vars_needed)) %>% droplevels()
}

md_cc3 <- make_md_cc3(all_data_clean)

length(unique(md_cc3$speaker))


# ----------------------------------------
# 3) lx-only model
# ----------------------------------------
mod4_full <- lmer(
  z_logdur ~ segment + prev_POA + nxt_POA +
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc3,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod4_final <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod4_full,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = "AIC",
  verbose = TRUE, # set TRUE if you want EVERY candidate printed
)

anova(mod4_final)

# ----------------------------------------
# 4) full model
# ----------------------------------------

mod5_full <- lmer(
  z_logdur ~ segment + prev_POA + nxt_POA +
    task + z_masc + z_femme + z_gn + segment * sexuality_gender_cut +
    race_ethn_cut + L1s_cut +
    place_origin_cut + socio_econ_cut + 
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc3,
  REML = FALSE
)
anova(mod5_full)

mod_step3 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod5_full,
  keep_terms = c("segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA"),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

mod_step3
anova(mod_step3)
saveRDS(mod_step3, "mod_step_dur_all.rds")
r2_nakagawa(mod_step3)


### /s/-only ###
md_cc3_s <- md_cc3[md_cc3$segment == "S", ]
length(unique(md_cc3_s$speaker))

mod4_full1 <- lmer(
   z_logdur ~ prev_POA + nxt_POA + 
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc3_s,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod4_final1 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod4_full1,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod4_final1)


mod_dur_s <- lmer(z_logdur ~ prev_POA + nxt_POA +
    task + z_masc + z_femme + z_gn + sexuality_gender_cut +
    race_ethn_cut + L1s_cut + place_origin_cut + socio_econ_cut + 
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc3_s,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod_step_s3 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod_dur_s,
  keep_terms = c("prev_POA","nxt_POA"),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod_step_s3)
saveRDS(mod_step_s3, "mod_step_dur_s.rds")

mod_step_s2 <- readRDS("mod_step_dur_s.rds") 
mod_step_s2
r2_nakagawa(mod_step_s2)
```

## Fm

```{r finmodfm}
exclude_speakers <- c("S18", "S44", "S09", "S10")

MPA_clean <- MPA %>%
  filter(!speaker %in% exclude_speakers)

length(unique(MPA_clean$speaker))

# ----------------------------
# 1) Build modeling dataset
# ----------------------------
make_md_cc4 <- function(MPA_clean) {

  # global medians for the three scalar predictors
  global_masc_med  <- median(as.numeric(MPA_clean$masc), na.rm = TRUE)
  global_femme_med <- median(as.numeric(MPA_clean$femme), na.rm = TRUE)
  global_gn_med    <- median(as.numeric(MPA_clean$gender_normative), na.rm = TRUE)

  md <- MPA_clean %>%
    mutate(
      # coerce to numeric
      masc  = as.numeric(masc),
      femme = as.numeric(femme),
      gender_normative = as.numeric(gender_normative),

      # global-median imputation (ONLY these three)
      masc  = if_else(is.na(masc),  global_masc_med,  masc),
      femme = if_else(is.na(femme), global_femme_med, femme),
      gender_normative = if_else(is.na(gender_normative), global_gn_med, gender_normative)
    ) %>%
    transmute(
      Fm,
      logFm = log(Fm),
      duration,

      # linguistic
      segment,
      prev_POA,
      nxt_POA,

      # social (categorical)
      task,
      place_origin_cut,
      L1s_cut,
      socio_econ_cut,
      race_ethn_cut,
      sexuality_gender_cut,

      # numeric-only predictors (imputed)
      masc,
      femme,
      gender_normative,

      # grouping factors
      word,
      speaker
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      # z-score numeric-only
      z_dur   = as.numeric(scale(duration)),
      z_masc  = as.numeric(scale(masc)),
      z_femme = as.numeric(scale(femme)),
      z_gn    = as.numeric(scale(gender_normative)),

      word    = droplevels(factor(word)),
      speaker = droplevels(factor(speaker))
    ) %>%
    droplevels()

  # sum-code fixed-effect factors (NOT word/speaker)
  factor_cols <- c(
    "segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","task",
    "place_origin_cut","L1s_cut","socio_econ_cut",
    "race_ethn_cut","sexuality_gender_cut"
  )
  md <- sum_code(md, factor_cols)

  # rows required for modeling
  vars_needed <- c(
    "logFm","z_dur","z_masc","z_femme","z_gn",
    "segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","task",
    "place_origin_cut","L1s_cut","socio_econ_cut","race_ethn_cut","sexuality_gender_cut",
    "word","speaker"
  )

  md %>% tidyr::drop_na(dplyr::all_of(vars_needed)) %>% droplevels()
}

md_cc4 <- make_md_cc4(MPA_clean)

length(unique(md_cc4$speaker))


# ----------------------------------------
# 3) lx-only model
# ----------------------------------------
mod6_full <- lmer(
  logFm ~ segment + prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc4,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod6_final <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod6_full,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = "AIC",
  verbose = TRUE, # set TRUE if you want EVERY candidate printed
)

anova(mod6_final)

# ----------------------------------------
# 4) full model
# ----------------------------------------

mod7_full <- lmer(
  logFm ~ segment + prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    task + z_masc + z_femme + z_gn + segment * sexuality_gender_cut +
    race_ethn_cut + L1s_cut +
    place_origin_cut + socio_econ_cut + 
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc4,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod_step4 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod7_full,
  keep_terms = c("segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","z_dur"),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

mod_step4
anova(mod_step4)
saveRDS(mod_step4, "mod_step_Fm_all.rds") 
r2_nakagawa(mod_step4)


### /s/-only ###
md_cc4_s <- md_cc4[md_cc4$segment == "S", ]
length(unique(md_cc4_s$speaker))

mod6_full1 <- lmer(
  logFm ~ prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc4_s,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod6_final1 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod6_full1,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod6_final1)


mod_fm_s <- lmer(logFm ~ prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    task + z_masc + z_femme + z_gn + sexuality_gender_cut +
    race_ethn_cut + L1s_cut + place_origin_cut + socio_econ_cut + 
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc4_s,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod_step_s4 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod_fm_s,
  keep_terms = c("prev_POA","nxt_POA","z_dur"),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod_step_s4)
saveRDS(mod_step_s4, "mod_step_fm_s.rds") 

mod_step_s4 <- readRDS("mod_step_fm_s.rds") 
mod_step_s4
r2_nakagawa(mod_step_s4)
```

## Am

```{r finmodam}
# ----------------------------
# 1) Build modeling dataset
# ----------------------------
make_md_cc5 <- function(MPA_clean) {

  # global medians for the three scalar predictors
  global_masc_med  <- median(as.numeric(MPA_clean$masc), na.rm = TRUE)
  global_femme_med <- median(as.numeric(MPA_clean$femme), na.rm = TRUE)
  global_gn_med    <- median(as.numeric(MPA_clean$gender_normative), na.rm = TRUE)

  md <- MPA_clean %>%
    mutate(
      # coerce to numeric
      masc  = as.numeric(masc),
      femme = as.numeric(femme),
      gender_normative = as.numeric(gender_normative),

      # global-median imputation (ONLY these three)
      masc  = if_else(is.na(masc),  global_masc_med,  masc),
      femme = if_else(is.na(femme), global_femme_med, femme),
      gender_normative = if_else(is.na(gender_normative), global_gn_med, gender_normative)
    ) %>%
    transmute(
      Am,
      duration,

      # linguistic
      segment,
      prev_POA,
      nxt_POA,

      # social (categorical)
      task,
      place_origin_cut,
      L1s_cut,
      socio_econ_cut,
      race_ethn_cut,
      sexuality_gender_cut,

      # numeric-only predictors (imputed)
      masc,
      femme,
      gender_normative,

      # grouping factors
      word,
      speaker
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      # z-score numeric-only
      z_dur   = as.numeric(scale(duration)),
      z_masc  = as.numeric(scale(masc)),
      z_femme = as.numeric(scale(femme)),
      z_gn    = as.numeric(scale(gender_normative)),
      z_Am = as.numeric(scale(Am)),

      word    = droplevels(factor(word)),
      speaker = droplevels(factor(speaker))
    ) %>%
    droplevels()

  # sum-code fixed-effect factors (NOT word/speaker)
  factor_cols <- c(
    "segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","task",
    "place_origin_cut","L1s_cut","socio_econ_cut",
    "race_ethn_cut","sexuality_gender_cut"
  )
  md <- sum_code(md, factor_cols)

  # rows required for modeling
  vars_needed <- c(
    "z_Am","z_dur","z_masc","z_femme","z_gn",
    "segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","task",
    "place_origin_cut","L1s_cut","socio_econ_cut","race_ethn_cut","sexuality_gender_cut",
    "word","speaker"
  )

  md %>% tidyr::drop_na(dplyr::all_of(vars_needed)) %>% droplevels()
}

md_cc5 <- make_md_cc5(MPA_clean)

length(unique(md_cc5$speaker))


# ----------------------------------------
# 3) lx-only model
# ----------------------------------------
mod7_full <- lmer(
  z_Am ~ segment + prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc5,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod7_final <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod7_full,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = "AIC",
  verbose = TRUE, # set TRUE if you want EVERY candidate printed
)

anova(mod7_final)

# ----------------------------------------
# 4) full model
# ----------------------------------------

mod8_full <- lmer(
  z_Am ~ segment + z_dur +
    task + z_masc + z_femme + z_gn + segment * sexuality_gender_cut +
    race_ethn_cut + L1s_cut +
    place_origin_cut + socio_econ_cut + 
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc5,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod_step5 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod8_full,
  keep_terms = c("segment","z_dur"),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

mod_step5
anova(mod_step5)
saveRDS(mod_step5, "mod_step_Am_all.rds") 
r2_nakagawa(mod_step5)


### /s/-only ###
md_cc5_s <- md_cc5[md_cc5$segment == "S", ]
length(unique(md_cc5_s$speaker))

mod12_full1 <- lmer(
  z_Am ~ prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc5_s,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod12_final1 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod12_full1,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod12_final1)


mod_am_s <- lmer(z_Am ~ nxt_POA + z_dur +
    task + z_masc + z_femme + z_gn + sexuality_gender_cut +
    race_ethn_cut + L1s_cut + place_origin_cut + socio_econ_cut + 
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc5_s,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod_step_s5 <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod_am_s,
  keep_terms = c("prev_POA","nxt_POA","z_dur"),
  criterion = "AIC",
  max_steps = 50,
  max_drop_size = 4,          # IMPORTANT: set a cap unless droppable terms are small
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE
)

anova(mod_step_s5)
saveRDS(mod_step_s5, "mod_step_am_s.rds") 

mod_step_s5 <- readRDS("mod_step_am_s.rds") 
mod_step_s5
r2_nakagawa(mod_step_s5)

```

## F1

```{r gridsearch}
capture_fit <- function(expr) {
  msgs  <- character()
  warns <- character()
  val <- withCallingHandlers(
    expr,
    message = function(m) { msgs  <<- c(msgs,  conditionMessage(m)); invokeRestart("muffleMessage") },
    warning = function(w) { warns <<- c(warns, conditionMessage(w)); invokeRestart("muffleWarning") }
  )
  list(value = val, messages = msgs, warnings = warns)
}

grid_search_lmer_AIC_light <- function(
  data,
  response,
  keep_terms = c("segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA"),
  optional_main = c("task","z_masc","z_femme","z_gn"),
  interaction_terms = c("sexuality_gender_cut","race_ethn_cut","L1s_cut","place_origin_cut","socio_econ_cut"),
  re_terms = c("(1|word)", "(1|speaker)"),
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE,
  verbose = TRUE,
  keep_best_fit = TRUE,      # keep only the best model object
  keep_top_k = 0,            # optionally keep top K fits (0 = none)
  gc_every = 10              # run gc() every N models
) {
  switches <- c(optional_main, interaction_terms)
  int_map  <- setNames(paste0("segment:", interaction_terms), interaction_terms)

  build_formula_text <- function(state_vec) {
    terms <- unique(keep_terms)

    # optional mains
    for (v in optional_main) if (isTRUE(state_vec[[v]])) terms <- c(terms, v)

    # interaction blocks (+ main effect if enforce_hierarchy)
    for (x in interaction_terms) {
      if (isTRUE(state_vec[[x]])) {
        if (enforce_hierarchy) terms <- c(terms, x)
        terms <- c(terms, int_map[[x]])
      }
    }

    rhs <- paste(unique(terms), collapse = " + ")
    rhs <- paste(rhs, paste(re_terms, collapse = " + "), sep = " + ")
    paste0(response, " ~ ", rhs)
  }

  # enumerate all 2^p combinations (p = 9 => 512 models)
  p <- length(switches)
  grid_mat <- as.data.frame(expand.grid(rep(list(c(FALSE, TRUE)), p)))
  names(grid_mat) <- switches
  n <- nrow(grid_mat)

  if (verbose) cat(sprintf("Grid size: %d models (%d switches)\n", n, p))

  # results we store (small!)
  res_ok     <- logical(n)
  res_AIC    <- rep(Inf, n)
  res_form   <- character(n)
  res_conv   <- character(n)
  res_error  <- character(n)

  # best tracking
  best_AIC <- Inf
  best_fit <- NULL
  best_formula <- NA_character_

  # optionally keep top-K fits (still bounded memory)
  top_fits <- list()
  top_AICs <- numeric(0)
  top_forms <- character(0)

  ctrl <- lme4::lmerControl(calc.derivs = FALSE)

  for (i in seq_len(n)) {
    state_i <- as.list(grid_mat[i, , drop = FALSE])
    ftxt <- build_formula_text(state_i)
    res_form[i] <- ftxt

    if (verbose) {
      cat(sprintf("\n[%d/%d]\n%s\n", i, n, ftxt))
    }

    fit_res <- capture_fit(
      tryCatch(lme4::lmer(stats::as.formula(ftxt), data = data, REML = FALSE, control = ctrl),
               error = function(e) e)
    )

    ok <- inherits(fit_res$value, "merMod")
    res_ok[i] <- ok

    if (ok) {
      fit <- fit_res$value
      aic <- AIC(fit)
      res_AIC[i] <- aic

      # convergence messages if any
      conv <- NA_character_
      optinfo <- fit@optinfo
      if (!is.null(optinfo$conv$lme4$messages)) {
        conv <- paste(optinfo$conv$lme4$messages, collapse = " | ")
      }
      res_conv[i] <- conv

      # update best (and optionally keep the best fit object)
      if (aic + 1e-9 < best_AIC) {
        best_AIC <- aic
        best_formula <- ftxt
        if (keep_best_fit) best_fit <- fit
        if (verbose) cat(sprintf(">> NEW BEST AIC: %.3f\n", best_AIC))
      }

      # optionally maintain a bounded list of top-K fits
      if (keep_top_k > 0) {
        # insert
        top_AICs <- c(top_AICs, aic)
        top_forms <- c(top_forms, ftxt)
        if (keep_best_fit) top_fits <- c(top_fits, list(fit)) else top_fits <- top_fits

        # keep only top K by AIC
        ord <- order(top_AICs)
        if (length(ord) > keep_top_k) ord <- ord[seq_len(keep_top_k)]
        top_AICs <- top_AICs[ord]
        top_forms <- top_forms[ord]
        if (keep_best_fit) top_fits <- top_fits[ord]
      }

      # IMPORTANT: if we are not keeping this fit, drop it immediately
      if (!keep_best_fit && keep_top_k == 0) {
        rm(fit)
      }
    } else {
      res_error[i] <- conditionMessage(fit_res$value)
    }

    if (gc_every > 0 && (i %% gc_every == 0)) {
      gc()
    }
  }

  results <- cbind(
    grid_mat,
    data.frame(
      ok = res_ok,
      AIC = res_AIC,
      formula = res_form,
      conv = res_conv,
      error = res_error,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  )
  results <- results[order(results$AIC), ]

  list(
    results = results,
    best_AIC = best_AIC,
    best_formula = best_formula,
    best_model = best_fit,
    top_AICs = if (keep_top_k > 0) top_AICs else NULL,
    top_formulas = if (keep_top_k > 0) top_forms else NULL,
    top_models = if (keep_top_k > 0 && keep_best_fit) top_fits else NULL
  )
}

```

```{r finmodf1}

exclude_speakers <- c("S18", "S44", "S09", "S10")

norm_formants_clean <- norm_formants %>%
  filter(!speaker %in% exclude_speakers)

length(unique(norm_formants_clean$speaker))

# ----------------------------
# 1) Build modeling dataset
# ----------------------------
make_md_cc7 <- function(norm_formants_clean) {

  # global medians for the three scalar predictors
  global_masc_med  <- median(as.numeric(norm_formants_clean$masc), na.rm = TRUE)
  global_femme_med <- median(as.numeric(norm_formants_clean$femme), na.rm = TRUE)
  global_gn_med    <- median(as.numeric(norm_formants_clean$gender_normative), na.rm = TRUE)

  md <- norm_formants_clean %>%
    mutate(
      # coerce to numeric
      masc  = as.numeric(masc),
      femme = as.numeric(femme),
      gender_normative = as.numeric(gender_normative),

      # global-median imputation (ONLY these three)
      masc  = if_else(is.na(masc),  global_masc_med,  masc),
      femme = if_else(is.na(femme), global_femme_med, femme),
      gender_normative = if_else(is.na(gender_normative), global_gn_med, gender_normative)
    ) %>%
    transmute(
      F1_norm,
      F2_norm,
      duration,

      # linguistic
      segment,
      prev_POA,
      nxt_POA,

      # social (categorical)
      task,
      place_origin_cut,
      L1s_cut,
      socio_econ_cut,
      race_ethn_cut,
      sexuality_gender_cut,

      # numeric-only predictors (imputed)
      masc,
      femme,
      gender_normative,

      # grouping factors
      word,
      speaker
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      # z-score numeric-only
      z_dur   = as.numeric(scale(duration)),
      z_masc  = as.numeric(scale(masc)),
      z_femme = as.numeric(scale(femme)),
      z_gn    = as.numeric(scale(gender_normative)),
      z_F1_norm = as.numeric(scale(F1_norm)),
      z_F2_norm = as.numeric(scale(F2_norm)),

      word    = droplevels(factor(word)),
      speaker = droplevels(factor(speaker))
    ) %>%
    droplevels()

  # sum-code fixed-effect factors (NOT word/speaker)
  factor_cols <- c(
    "segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","task",
    "place_origin_cut","L1s_cut","socio_econ_cut",
    "race_ethn_cut","sexuality_gender_cut"
  )
  md <- sum_code(md, factor_cols)

  # rows required for modeling
  vars_needed <- c(
    "z_F1_norm", "z_F2_norm","z_dur","z_masc","z_femme","z_gn",
    "segment","prev_POA","nxt_POA","task",
    "place_origin_cut","L1s_cut","socio_econ_cut","race_ethn_cut","sexuality_gender_cut",
    "word","speaker"
  )

  md %>% tidyr::drop_na(dplyr::all_of(vars_needed)) %>% droplevels()
}

md_cc7 <- make_md_cc7(norm_formants_clean)

length(unique(md_cc7$speaker))


# ----------------------------------------
# 3) lx-only model
# ----------------------------------------
mod13_full <- lmer(
  z_F1_norm ~ segment + prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc7,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod13_final <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod13_full,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = "AIC",
  verbose = TRUE, # set TRUE if you want EVERY candidate printed
)

anova(mod13_final)
mod13_final

# ----------------------------------------
# 4) full model
# ----------------------------------------

gs_F1 <- grid_search_lmer_AIC_light(
  data = md_cc7,
  response = "z_F1_norm",

  # always-on (your base linguistic block)
  keep_terms = c("segment", "prev_POA", "nxt_POA", "z_dur"),

  # OPTIONAL mains (no interactions for these)
  optional_main = c(
    "task", "z_masc", "z_femme", "z_gn",
    "race_ethn_cut", "L1s_cut", "place_origin_cut", "socio_econ_cut"
  ),

  # ONLY interaction block tested (and includes main effect if enforce_hierarchy=TRUE)
  interaction_terms = c("sexuality_gender_cut"),

  re_terms = c("(1|word)", "(1|speaker)"),
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE,
  verbose = TRUE,
  keep_best_fit = TRUE,
  keep_top_k = 0,
  gc_every = 10
)

head(gs_F1$results, 20)
gs_F1$best_AIC
gs_F1$best_formula
gs_F1

# ANOVA on the best model (only if kept)
mod_step6 <- lmer(
  as.formula(gs_F1$best_formula),
  data = md_cc7,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod_step6
anova(mod_step6)
saveRDS(mod_step6, "mod_step_F1_all.rds") 
r2_nakagawa(mod_step6)
```

## F2
```{r finmodf2}
# ----------------------------------------
# 3) lx-only model
# ----------------------------------------
mod10_full <- lmer(
  z_F2_norm ~ segment + prev_POA + nxt_POA + z_dur +
    (1 | word) + (1 | speaker),
  data = md_cc7,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod10_final <- stepwise_lmer_best_subset_print_all(
  mod10_full,
  keep_terms = character(0),
  criterion = "AIC",
  verbose = TRUE, # set TRUE if you want EVERY candidate printed
)

anova(mod10_final)

# ----------------------------------------
# 4) full model
# ----------------------------------------

gs_F2 <- grid_search_lmer_AIC_light(
  data = md_cc7,
  response = "z_F2_norm",

  # always-on (your base linguistic block)
  keep_terms = c("segment", "prev_POA", "nxt_POA", "z_dur"),

  # OPTIONAL mains (no interactions for these)
  optional_main = c(
    "task", "z_masc", "z_femme", "z_gn",
    "race_ethn_cut", "L1s_cut", "place_origin_cut", "socio_econ_cut"
  ),

  # ONLY interaction block tested (and includes main effect if enforce_hierarchy=TRUE)
  interaction_terms = c("sexuality_gender_cut"),

  re_terms = c("(1|word)", "(1|speaker)"),
  enforce_hierarchy = TRUE,
  verbose = TRUE,
  keep_best_fit = TRUE,
  keep_top_k = 0,
  gc_every = 10
)

head(gs_F2$results, 20)
gs_F2$best_AIC
gs_F2$best_formula
gs_F2

# ANOVA on the best model (only if kept)
mod_step7 <- lmer(
  as.formula(gs_F2$best_formula),
  data = md_cc7,
  REML = FALSE
)

mod_step7
anova(mod_step7)
saveRDS(mod_step7, "mod_step_F2_all.rds") 
r2_nakagawa(mod_step7)
```


# Get pairwise results
## COG
```{r pairwisecog}
anova(mod_step)
options(max.print = 1e7)
em_cog2 <- emmeans(
  mod_step_s,
  pairwise ~ sexuality_gender_cut,
  adjust = "bonferroni",
  lmer.df = "satterthwaite",
  pbkrtest.limit = 46000,
  lmerTest.limit = 46000,
  rg.limit = 550000
)

saveRDS(em_cog2, "em_cog_s_sexuality_gender_main.rds")        # save object
capture.output(summary(em_cog2),           # save readable version
               file="em_cog_s_sexuality_gender_main.txt")
```

## SD
```{r pairwisesd}
mod_step1
options(max.print = 1e7)
em_sd3 <- emmeans(
  mod_step1,
  pairwise ~ task,
  adjust = "bonferroni",
  lmer.df = "satterthwaite",
  pbkrtest.limit = 46000,
  lmerTest.limit = 46000,
  rg.limit = 550000
)

saveRDS(em_sd3, "em_sd_task.rds")        # save object
capture.output(summary(em_sd3),           # save readable version
               file="em_sd_task.txt")
```

## skew
```{r pairwiseskew}
mod_step2
options(max.print = 1e7)
em_skew <- emmeans(
  mod_step2,
  pairwise ~ task,
  adjust = "bonferroni",
  lmer.df = "satterthwaite",
  pbkrtest.limit = 46000,
  lmerTest.limit = 46000,
  rg.limit = 550000
)

saveRDS(em_skew, "em_skew_task.rds")        # save object
capture.output(summary(em_skew),           # save readable version
               file="em_skew_task.txt")
```

## duration
```{r pairwisedur}
anova(mod_step3)
options(max.print = 1e7)
em_dur3 <- emmeans(
  mod_step3,
  pairwise ~ task,
  adjust = "bonferroni",
  lmer.df = "satterthwaite",
  pbkrtest.limit = 46000,
  lmerTest.limit = 46000,
  rg.limit = 550000
)

saveRDS(em_dur3, "em_dur_task.rds")        # save object
capture.output(summary(em_dur3),           # save readable version
               file="em_dur_task.txt")
```

## Fm
```{r pairwisefm}
anova(mod_step3)
options(max.print = 1e7)
em_Fm <- emmeans(
  mod_step4,
  pairwise ~ segment:sexuality_gender_cut,
  adjust = "bonferroni",
  lmer.df = "satterthwaite",
  pbkrtest.limit = 46000,
  lmerTest.limit = 46000,
  rg.limit = 550000
)

saveRDS(em_Fm, "em_Fm_sexuality_gender.rds")        # save object
capture.output(summary(em_Fm),           # save readable version
               file="em_Fm_sexuality_gender.txt")
```

## Am
```{r pairwiseam}
anova(mod_step5)
options(max.print = 1e7)
em_Am4 <- emmeans(
  mod_step_s5,
  pairwise ~ task,
  adjust = "bonferroni",
  lmer.df = "satterthwaite",
  pbkrtest.limit = 46000,
  lmerTest.limit = 46000,
  rg.limit = 550000
)

saveRDS(em_Am4, "em_Am_task_s.rds")        # save object
capture.output(summary(em_Am4),           # save readable version
               file="em_Am_task_s.txt")
```

## F1
```{r pairwiseam}
gs_F1$best_formula
options(max.print = 1e7)
em_f14 <- emmeans(
  mod_step6,
  pairwise ~ task,
  adjust = "bonferroni",
  lmer.df = "satterthwaite",
  pbkrtest.limit = 58000,
  lmerTest.limit = 58000,
  rg.limit = 550000
)

saveRDS(em_f14, "em_f1_task.rds")        # save object
capture.output(summary(em_f14),           # save readable version
               file="em_f1_task.txt")
```

## F2
```{r pairwiseam}
anova(mod_step5)
options(max.print = 1e7)
em_f2 <- emmeans(
  mod_step7,
  pairwise ~ segment:sexuality_gender_cut,
  adjust = "bonferroni",
  lmer.df = "satterthwaite",
  pbkrtest.limit = 46000,
  lmerTest.limit = 46000,
  rg.limit = 550000
)

saveRDS(em_f2, "em_f2_sexuality_gender.rds")        # save object
capture.output(summary(em_f2),           # save readable version
               file="em_f2_sexuality_gender.txt")
```

# Plot trend for Likert scales
##SD
```{r Likert}
# -------------------------
# GENDER NORMATIVITY: map Likert 1:7 -> z_gn using md_cc1
# -------------------------
# Compute the mean and SD of the original gender-normativity variable
# so 1–7 raw Likert values can be mapped onto the z-scored scale used in the model.
# R mean / sd docs:
# https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/base/html/mean.html
# https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/stats/html/sd.html
gn_mean <- mean(md_cc1$gender_normative, na.rm = TRUE)
gn_sd   <- sd(md_cc1$gender_normative, na.rm = TRUE)

# Raw Likert values to display on the plot
likert_vals <- 1:7

# Convert raw Likert values to the z-score scale used by the fitted model
z_gn_vals   <- (likert_vals - gn_mean) / gn_sd

# Estimated marginal means at those z-scored values.
# emmeans computes model-based estimated marginal means (least-squares means)
# and accepts specific covariate values via the 'at' argument.
# emmeans docs:
# https://rvlenth.github.io/emmeans/reference/emmeans.html
emm_gn <- emmeans(
  mod_step1,
  ~ z_gn,
  at = list(z_gn = z_gn_vals),
  lmer.df = "satterthwaite",
  adjust = "bonferroni",
  pbkrtest.limit = 3790000,
  lmerTest.limit = 3790000,
  rg.limit = 3790000
)

# Convert the emmGrid result to a data frame and attach both the raw Likert
# values and their z-scored counterparts for plotting.
# summary / as.data.frame methods for emmGrid:
# https://rvlenth.github.io/emmeans/reference/summary.emmGrid.html
# dplyr mutate:
# https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/mutate.html
emm_gn_df <- as.data.frame(emm_gn) %>%
  mutate(
    gn_raw = likert_vals,
    z_gn   = z_gn_vals
  )

# Raw plotting data:
# use logSD if it already exists; otherwise create it from SD.
# dplyr mutate / filter:
# https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/mutate.html
# https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/reference/filter.html
plot_gn_df <- md_cc1 %>%
  mutate(
    logSD = if ("logSD" %in% names(.)) logSD else log(SD)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(gender_normative), !is.na(logSD))

# Build plot:
# - jittered raw observations
# - model-based confidence ribbon
# - EMM line and points
#
# ggplot2 docs:
# geom_jitter: https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/geom_jitter.html
# geom_ribbon: https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/geom_ribbon.html
# geom_line / geom_path: https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/geom_path.html
# geom_point: https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/geom_point.html
# scale_x_continuous: https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/scale_continuous.html
# labs: https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/labs.html
# theme_classic: https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/ggtheme.html
p_gn <- ggplot() +
  geom_jitter(
    data = plot_gn_df,
    aes(x = gender_normative, y = logSD),
    width = 0.15,
    alpha = 0.2,
    size = 1.5
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = emm_gn_df,
    aes(x = gn_raw, ymin = lower.CL, ymax = upper.CL),
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = emm_gn_df,
    aes(x = gn_raw, y = emmean),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = emm_gn_df,
    aes(x = gn_raw, y = emmean),
    size = 2
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:7, limits = c(1, 7)) +
  labs(
    x = "Gender normativity (1–7 Likert)",
    y = "log(SD)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14)

# Save the plot to disk.
# ggsave docs:
# https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/ggsave.html
ggsave(
  filename = "gn_plot_logSD.png",
  plot = p_gn,
  width = 6,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 300
)

```

## Fm

```{r Likert1}
# -------------------------
# FEMME: map Likert 1:7 -> z_femme using md_cc7
# -------------------------
femme_mean <- mean(md_cc4$femme, na.rm = TRUE)
femme_sd   <- sd(md_cc4$femme, na.rm = TRUE)

likert_vals <- 1:7
z_femme_vals <- (likert_vals - femme_mean) / femme_sd

# EMMEANS at those z values
emm_femme <- emmeans(
  best_fit_f1,
  ~ z_femme,
  at = list(z_femme = z_femme_vals),
  adjust = "bonferroni",
  lmer.df = "satterthwaite",
  pbkrtest.limit = 3790000,
  lmerTest.limit = 3790000,
  rg.limit = 3790000
)

emm_femme_df <- as.data.frame(emm_femme)
emm_femme_df$femme_raw <- likert_vals

# Raw plotting data (only rows with needed columns present)
plot_femme_df <- md_cc4[!is.na(md_cc4$femme) &
                        !is.na(md_cc4$z_F1_norm), ]

p_femme <- ggplot() +
  # raw data
  geom_jitter(
    data = plot_femme_df,
    aes(x = femme, y = z_F1_norm),
    width = 0.15,
    alpha = 0.2,
    size = 1.5
  ) +
  # CI ribbon
  geom_ribbon(
    data = emm_femme_df,
    aes(x = femme_raw, ymin = lower.CL, ymax = upper.CL),
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  # predicted line + points
  geom_line(
    data = emm_femme_df,
    aes(x = femme_raw, y = emmean),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = emm_femme_df,
    aes(x = femme_raw, y = emmean),
    size = 2
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:7, limits = c(1, 7)) +
  labs(
    x = "Femininity (1–7 Likert)",
    y = "scale(F1_norm)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14)

ggsave(
  filename = "femme_plot_logFm.png",
  plot = p_femme,
  width = 6,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 300
)

```

## F1
```{r Likert2}
# -------------------------
# FEMME: map Likert 1:7 -> z_femme using md_cc7
# -------------------------
femme_mean <- mean(md_cc7$femme, na.rm = TRUE)
femme_sd   <- sd(md_cc7$femme, na.rm = TRUE)

likert_vals <- 1:7
z_femme_vals <- (likert_vals - femme_mean) / femme_sd

# EMMEANS at those z values
emm_femme <- emmeans(
  mod_step7,
  ~ z_femme,
  at = list(z_femme = z_femme_vals),
  adjust = "bonferroni",
  lmer.df = "satterthwaite",
  pbkrtest.limit = 3790000,
  lmerTest.limit = 3790000,
  rg.limit = 3790000
)

emm_femme_df <- as.data.frame(emm_femme)
emm_femme_df$femme_raw <- likert_vals

# Raw plotting data (only rows with needed columns present)
plot_femme_df <- md_cc7[!is.na(md_cc7$femme) &
                        !is.na(md_cc7$z_F1_norm), ]

p_femme <- ggplot() +
  # raw data
  geom_jitter(
    data = plot_femme_df,
    aes(x = femme, y = z_F1_norm),
    width = 0.15,
    alpha = 0.2,
    size = 1.5
  ) +
  # CI ribbon
  geom_ribbon(
    data = emm_femme_df,
    aes(x = femme_raw, ymin = lower.CL, ymax = upper.CL),
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  # predicted line + points
  geom_line(
    data = emm_femme_df,
    aes(x = femme_raw, y = emmean),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = emm_femme_df,
    aes(x = femme_raw, y = emmean),
    size = 2
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:7, limits = c(1, 7)) +
  labs(
    x = "Femininity (1–7 Likert)",
    y = "scale(F1_norm)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14)

ggsave(
  filename = "femme_plot_zF1norm.png",
  plot = p_femme,
  width = 6,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 300
)

```

## F2
```{r Likert2}
# -------------------------
# FEMME: map Likert 1:7 -> z_femme using md_cc7
# -------------------------
femme_mean <- mean(md_cc7$femme, na.rm = TRUE)
femme_sd   <- sd(md_cc7$femme, na.rm = TRUE)

likert_vals <- 1:7
z_femme_vals <- (likert_vals - femme_mean) / femme_sd

# EMMEANS at those z values
emm_femme <- emmeans(
  mod_step7,
  ~ z_femme,
  at = list(z_femme = z_femme_vals),
  adjust = "bonferroni",
  lmer.df = "satterthwaite",
  pbkrtest.limit = 3790000,
  lmerTest.limit = 3790000,
  rg.limit = 3790000
)

emm_femme_df <- as.data.frame(emm_femme)
emm_femme_df$femme_raw <- likert_vals

# Raw plotting data (only rows with needed columns present)
plot_femme_df <- md_cc7[!is.na(md_cc7$femme) &
                        !is.na(md_cc7$z_F2_norm), ]

p_femme <- ggplot() +
  # raw data
  geom_jitter(
    data = plot_femme_df,
    aes(x = femme, y = z_F2_norm),
    width = 0.15,
    alpha = 0.2,
    size = 1.5
  ) +
  # CI ribbon
  geom_ribbon(
    data = emm_femme_df,
    aes(x = femme_raw, ymin = lower.CL, ymax = upper.CL),
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  # predicted line + points
  geom_line(
    data = emm_femme_df,
    aes(x = femme_raw, y = emmean),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = emm_femme_df,
    aes(x = femme_raw, y = emmean),
    size = 2
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:7, limits = c(1, 7)) +
  labs(
    x = "Femininity (1–7 Likert)",
    y = "scale(F2_norm)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14)

ggsave(
  filename = "femme_plot_zF2norm.png",
  plot = p_femme,
  width = 6,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 300
)

```
